engineIntelligence.h2: Porsche 928 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Porsche 928 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Porsche 928 M28.41 engine - petrol 5.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Thrust bearing failure - crankshaft axial play develops from 80k+ miles, causing knock and potential catastrophic damage.
Timing belt wear - interference engine; belt failure causes valve-to-piston contact requiring full rebuild.
Oil leak from cam cover gaskets - common on high-mileage units, leads to consumption and smoke.

cards.altText: Porsche 928 M28.49 engine - petrol 5.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Thrust bearing failure - same design vulnerability as earlier M28 engines; monitor for end-float knock.
Rear main seal leak - common on high-mileage GTS units; labour-intensive to address.
LH-Jetronic fuel injection faults - ageing sensors and wiring cause running issues on 30+ year-old systems.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £6,500-£11,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable M28.49 units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Porsche 928 M28.01 engine - petrol 4.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - early SOHC design; slack causes rattles and potential jump at high mileage.
Oil consumption from valve stem seals - typical on 40+ year-old units; blue smoke on overrun.
K-Jetronic fuel distributor issues - mechanical injection requires specialist calibration; hard starting common.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £2,500-£5,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable early M28 units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Porsche 928 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Porsche 928 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.5L Petrol
entries.history: Original 928 powerplant; aluminium V8 with SOHC 16-valve design, establishing Porsche's front-engine GT philosophy.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - causes rattles and potential jump at high mileage on early units.
K-Jetronic fuel distributor faults - mechanical injection requires specialist calibration; hard starting common.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M28.01 Porsche 928 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.5L Petrol
entries.history: US-market evolution of the M28.01 with emissions equipment; same robust bottom end, reduced output.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - emissions equipment prone to carbon buildup affecting idle and performance.
Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets - common on ageing SOHC units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M28.03 Porsche 928 engine replacement

entries.title: 4.7L Petrol
entries.history: First major displacement increase; introduced for the S model with improved torque and revised cylinder heads.
compatibleVariants: 928 S (Euro manual), 928 S (Euro auto), 928 S (US manual), 928 S (US auto)
commonFailures: Thrust bearing wear - crankshaft axial play develops from 80k+ miles; listen for end-float knock.
Timing belt tensioner failure - interference engine; belt service critical every 4 years/40k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M28.11 Porsche 928 engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol
entries.history: Landmark 32-valve DOHC design for the S4; 5.0L displacement with 4 valves per cylinder, defining the modern 928.
compatibleVariants: 928 S4 (manual), 928 S4 (automatic), 928 S4 Club Sport, 928 S4 SE
commonFailures: Thrust bearing failure - most common catastrophic failure; budget for preventative inspection at 80k miles.
Cam cover gasket leaks - oil consumption and smoke on overrun common on high-mileage examples.
LH-Jetronic sensor faults - ageing MAF and temp sensors cause running issues on 30+ year-old systems.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M28.41 Porsche 928 engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol
entries.history: Performance-tuned variant for the GT; higher compression, revised cam profiles, and lightweight components.
commonFailures: Thrust bearing failure - same design vulnerability as S4; monitor for knock under load.
High-compression piston ring wear - increased cylinder pressure accelerates wear on neglected units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M28.47 Porsche 928 engine replacement

entries.title: 5.4L Petrol
entries.history: Final evolution of the M28; 5.4L displacement with revised block and heads for the ultimate GTS specification.
commonFailures: Thrust bearing failure - persistent design issue across all M28 engines; critical inspection point.
Rear main seal leaks - labour-intensive repair; common on high-mileage GTS units.
LH-Jetronic wiring harness degradation - brittle insulation on 30-year-old looms causes intermittent faults.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M28.49 Porsche 928 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Porsche 928 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Porsche 928's M28 V8 is robust but not immune to age-related failures. Thrust bearing wear, timing belt neglect, and oil leaks dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your 928.

problems.h4: Thrust Bearing Failure - Why Porsche 928 Engines Develop End-Float Knock
problems.rootCause: The crankshaft thrust washer wears over time, allowing axial movement that damages the bearing surface and creates a distinctive knocking noise under load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: End-float measurement, oil analysis, advisory report only
repairOptions.longevity: Not a repair; identifies risk. Suitable for low-mileage cars with early signs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, crankshaft inspection, new thrust bearing fitted, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses the failure point. Viable if block and internals are otherwise sound.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M28 unit with upgraded thrust components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution. Recommended for high-mileage or heavily worn engines.

problems.recommendation: On a Porsche 928 valued at £15,000-£40,000, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded thrust components offers the best balance of cost and longevity. If your car is lower mileage and otherwise pristine, an intermediate bearing replacement by a Porsche specialist may suffice-but budget for potential follow-on work.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M28.41/M28.49 engine prices for your Porsche 928

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Interference Engine Risk on Porsche 928 DOHC Units
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000 miles or 4 years since last change (whichever comes first)
problems.rootCause: The rubber timing belt degrades with age and heat; if it snaps, the interference design causes valves to strike pistons, resulting in catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New belt, tensioners, idlers, water pump (recommended), labour to access
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents failure. Essential every 4 years/40k miles. Best value if done proactively.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve replacement, head skim, new guides/seals, reassembly after belt snap
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block and pistons are undamaged. Risk of hidden damage remains.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt DOHC M28 unit, upgraded components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Safest option after catastrophic failure. Eliminates risk of residual damage.

problems.recommendation: Prevention is vastly cheaper than cure. If your belt is due or overdue, invest in a full kit change immediately. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine is usually more cost-effective and reliable than attempting a partial repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 928 S4 timing belt service or engine replacement

problems.h4: Oil Leak & Consumption - Common Sealing Issues on Ageing Porsche 928 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Porsche 928 all variants (1978-1995, all M28 codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000+ miles; accelerates with age and heat cycles
problems.rootCause: Ageing rubber gaskets (cam covers, rear main seal, sump) harden and shrink; valve stem seals wear, allowing oil into combustion chambers.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cam cover gaskets, sump seal, front crank seal replaced; accessible leaks only
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses visible leaks. Suitable for moderate consumption with no smoke.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Stem Seal & Rear Main Seal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head-off or specialist tool seal replacement; rear main seal addressed
repairOptions.longevity: Tackles consumption and major leaks. Viable if block is otherwise sound.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M28 unit with all-new seals, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution for high-mileage engines with multiple sealing failures.

problems.recommendation: For moderate leaks and light smoke, targeted seal replacement by a Porsche specialist offers good value. If consumption exceeds 1 litre per 1,000 miles or multiple seals are failing, a rebuilt engine often proves more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Compare Porsche 928 engine rebuild options for oil leak resolution

problems.h4: LH-Jetronic Fuel Injection Faults - Electrical Ageing on Late Porsche 928 Engines
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 25+ years of age; component failure not strictly mileage-dependent
problems.rootCause: Ageing wiring harness insulation becomes brittle; MAF sensors, temperature senders, and control unit capacitors degrade, causing erratic fueling and running issues.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fault code read, MAF/temp sensor replacement, basic wiring repair
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses specific faults. Suitable for intermittent issues with otherwise healthy engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full loom inspection, section replacement or re-sleeving, connector renewal
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive electrical refresh. Viable for concours or high-value examples.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M28 unit with refreshed ancillaries, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates injection uncertainty. Recommended if electrical faults compound mechanical wear.

problems.recommendation: Start with a specialist diagnostic to isolate the fault. Many LH-Jetronic issues are sensor-specific and cost-effective to fix. Only consider engine replacement if electrical faults are masking or compounding underlying mechanical wear.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 928 LH-Jetronic diagnostic or engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Porsche 928
finalCta.paragraph: The Porsche 928's M28 V8 is a masterpiece of engineering, but age and mileage take their toll. Whether you're facing thrust bearing knock, timing belt risk, or persistent oil leaks, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Porsche 928 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components to address known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and keep your classic GT on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Porsche 928 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Porsche 928 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Porsche 928 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Porsche 928 in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Porsche 928 4.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The original 4.5L M28.0x SOHC V8 powered the first-generation 928 from 1978-1979, establishing Porsche's front-engine GT formula with smooth, torquey character and aluminium construction.

items.title: Porsche 928 4.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced for the 928 S in 1980, the 4.7L M28.1x/2x series brought increased displacement and power, becoming the backbone of the mid-generation 928 range through 1986.
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption from valve stem seals

items.title: Porsche 928 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The landmark 5.0L DOHC 32-valve M28.4x series debuted in the 1987 S4, defining the modern 928 with significantly improved breathing, power and refinement for S4, GT and early GTS models.

items.title: Porsche 928 5.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The ultimate M28.49/50 5.4L V8 powered the final GTS from 1992-1995, offering peak displacement, torque and refinement for the last iteration of Porsche's classic front-engine grand tourer.

engineYears.h2: Porsche 928 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Porsche 928's 1978-1995 production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original 928 debuted with Porsche's first production V8: a 4.5L SOHC aluminium unit establishing the front-engine GT formula.
keyChanges: Launch of Porsche 928 with all-new M28.01 4.5L SOHC V8
Introduction of K-Jetronic mechanical fuel injection
Aluminium block and heads for weight savings and refinement
knownFor: Smooth, torquey character defining the classic GT experience
Timing chain tensioner wear on early units
years.cta: Get quotes for 1978-1979 Porsche 928 replacement engines

years.description: Displacement increased to 4.7L for the new S model, bringing significant power gains and establishing the M28.1x family.
keyChanges: Introduction of 4.7L M28.11 for 928 S model
Revised cylinder heads and intake for improved breathing
knownFor: Thrust bearing wear emerges as key failure point from 80k miles
Timing belt replacement critical every 4 years/40k miles
Strong torque character makes S a favoured grand tourer
years.cta: Get quotes for 1980-1982 Porsche 928 replacement engines

years.description: The S2 brought revised engine management and increased output to 310hp for Euro models, representing the peak of the SOHC M28 evolution.
keyChanges: Introduction of LH-Jetronic electronic injection on Euro S2
Power increased to 310hp for M28.21 Euro manual variant
Revised intake and exhaust for improved responsiveness
knownFor: Peak SOHC M28 performance and refinement
Thrust bearing monitoring essential on high-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 1984-1986 Porsche 928 replacement engines

years.description: The landmark S4 introduced the 5.0L DOHC 32-valve M28.41, transforming the 928 with significantly improved breathing and performance.
keyChanges: All-new 5.0L DOHC 32-valve M28.41 for S4 model
Power increased to 316-320hp with 4 valves per cylinder
Introduction of GT variant with performance-tuned M28.47 (330hp)
knownFor: Thrust bearing failure remains critical failure point across all DOHC units
Timing belt service absolutely critical; interference engine design
LH-Jetronic sensor and wiring ageing drives many replacement enquiries

years.ticker: Porsche 928 S4 engine replacement • Porsche 928 thrust bearing failure • Porsche 928 timing belt replacement cost

years.description: The ultimate 928 GTS received the enlarged 5.4L M28.49, delivering peak torque and refinement for the final chapter of the classic front-engine GT.
keyChanges: Introduction of 5.4L M28.49 for GTS model
Power increased to 345-350hp with revised block and heads
Final production year 1995 marks end of M28 engine family
knownFor: Peak M28 performance and refinement; highly collectible final edition
Thrust bearing and rear main seal leaks dominate high-mileage failure patterns
LH-Jetronic wiring harness degradation common on 30-year-old examples

faq.h2: Porsche 928 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Porsche 928 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Porsche 928 engine prices range from £2,500 for early 4.5L units to £11,000 for final 5.4L GTS engines, supply only. Supply & fit adds £1,200-£2,800 labour.
keyPoints: Early 4.5L M28.0x rebuilt: £2,500-£5,000 supply only
S4 5.0L M28.41 rebuilt: £4,500-£7,500 supply only
GTS 5.4L M28.49 rebuilt: £6,500-£11,000 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £1,200-£2,800
items.cta: Compare Porsche 928 engine replacement quotes today

items.question: What is the most reliable Porsche 928 engine code?
items.answer: The M28.21 (4.7L S2 Euro manual) is often cited as the most balanced for reliability and performance, though all M28 engines require thrust bearing monitoring.
keyPoints: M28.21 offers strong power with mature SOHC design
All M28 engines share thrust bearing vulnerability from 80k miles
Regular oil changes and timing belt service critical for longevity
DOHC M28.4x units demand stricter maintenance discipline
items.cta: Get quotes for M28.21 Porsche 928 engine replacement

items.question: Can I upgrade my Porsche 928 to a later engine code?
items.answer: Yes, S4/GTS engine swaps into earlier chassis are possible but require specialist expertise for ECU, wiring, and mounting compatibility.
keyPoints: M28.41 into pre-S4 chassis needs ECU and harness adaptation
Mounting points largely consistent across M28 family
Specialist fabrication may be needed for ancillaries and exhaust
Budget £1,500-£3,000 additional for integration work
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche 928 engine swap compatibility

items.question: How long does a Porsche 928 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch in 2-4 weeks; supply & fit installations require 3-5 days workshop time plus parts lead time.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines: 2-4 week build time from UK specialists
Used engines: 3-7 days if in stock with breaker network
Supply & fit installation: 3-5 days workshop labour
Allow extra time for ECU adaptation and road testing
items.cta: Book Porsche 928 engine supply & fit appointment

items.question: Do Porsche 928 replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; terms vary by supplier and engine condition.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/remanufactured: typically 12-month unlimited mileage
Reconditioned: 12-month on replaced components
Used: typically 3-6 months; confirm scope with supplier
Warranty covers parts; labour coverage varies by supply & fit agreement

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned, and remanufactured Porsche 928 engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines are fully stripped and upgraded to address known failures; reconditioned focuses on worn parts; remanufactured matches original factory specifications.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: full strip, upgraded thrust/timing components, best value
Reconditioned: clean, measure, replace worn parts only
Remanufactured: factory-spec tolerances, new/OEM parts throughout
All include warranty; choose based on budget and intended use
items.cta: Compare Porsche 928 rebuilt vs reconditioned engine options

items.question: Will a replacement Porsche 928 engine pass MOT emissions?
items.answer: Yes, if the replacement matches the original specification and catalytic converter/O2 sensors are functional; specialists ensure compliance during fitment.
keyPoints: Engine code must match original for emissions certification
Catalytic converter and O2 sensors must be operational
LH-Jetronic systems require correct calibration for clean running
Rebuilt engines from UK specialists are configured for MOT compliance
items.cta: Get quotes for MOT-compliant Porsche 928 engine replacement

items.question: How do I find my Porsche 928 engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the block near the distributor or on the VIN plate; enter your registration above for instant DVLA verification.
keyPoints: Locate stamp on engine block near distributor drive
Cross-reference with VIN plate in engine bay or door jamb
M28.xx format: e.g., M28.41 for S4, M28.49 for GTS
Our registration lookup instantly confirms code and compatibility
items.cta: Use our Porsche 928 engine code lookup tool

items.question: Should I repair my Porsche 928 engine or replace it?
items.answer: If thrust bearing knock, timing belt failure, or multiple major leaks are present, replacement often proves more cost-effective than incremental repairs.
keyPoints: Thrust bearing failure: replacement usually better value than bearing-only repair
Timing belt snap: rebuilt engine avoids hidden valve/piston damage risk
Multiple oil leaks: comprehensive rebuild addresses all sealing points at once
Get comparative quotes before deciding; factor in car value and intended use
items.cta: Compare Porsche 928 engine repair vs replacement costs

items.question: Are Porsche 928 engines still available for purchase in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt, reconditioned and used Porsche 928 engines are available through our UK specialist network; availability varies by engine code and condition.
keyPoints: M28.41 (S4) and M28.49 (GTS) have strongest specialist rebuild support
Used engines depend on breaker network stock; act quickly when available
Lead times: 2-4 weeks for rebuilt, 3-7 days for in-stock used units
All suppliers vetted for quality and warranty coverage
